title: The difficult part about living in a van - request

transcript.text: Hello, this video is the difficult part about living in a van.
Somebody asked me that. I don't know if they asked me to do a video about it, but I think it's a good object for a video.
Because I'm kind of having a difficult time right now, and it's related to living out of a van.
The difficult thing about living out of a van is going to be totally different, depending on what kind of a personality you have.
Um, it's, um, like for me, my personality is the personality of the worker, uh, the antisocial worker.
So if my work gets, uh, impeded, I'm not going to be happy.
Um, and my, and the way that living in a van can impede your work is, like with me, I don't have juice.
I don't have electric power in my van because it's fucking like rainy all goddamn day.
And there's only like four hours of daylight.
So my, my, my solar panel can't charge.
And I had my car on all day today, or for an hour, connected to my RV battery back here.
And, um, you know, I could, I could plug in just fine while the car was running.
But after an hour of being connected to the RV battery while the car was running, it only gave my RV battery 15 minutes on my computer.
I'm actually plugged into my RV battery right now, which is my video camera.
So we're going to see how long this lasts.
Hopefully it lasts a little while so I can make some videos before it goes beep.
And then I'm like, oh, fuck.
Then I have to, um, unplug it.
And then I can, I guess, uh, talk a little bit.
But my, my camera can't charge while it's being plugged in.
While it's turned on, it can only charge while it's turned off.
So I don't know if I'll be able to talk.
Because the battery's about to die and it's plugged in but not charging.
So, hopefully I'll get some time.
But, um, yeah, so I can't work in my car.
So at night, I have to be able to sleep at night or else I'm not going to get anything done.
You know, I can't work on my computer at night.
I could work on my netbook on my website, but I keep forgetting to transfer it over to my netbook.
And maybe I'll do that, but I need internet too.
To work on my site and there's no internet.
In that, uh, Walgreens, Walmart parking lot.
There is in the, uh, Safeway parking lot.
And it is a 24-hour Safeway, but I don't want to park there because Safeways aren't.
I didn't ask him if I could.
Maybe I should.
But, um, yeah, I guess theoretically I could work on my site on the Safeway on my netbook.
But I'd prefer to work on my notebook.
I mean, on my, my laptop.
It's bigger.
I like having a big screen.
And I don't like bifurcating my site.
Because I like just jumping around and working on lots of different pages.
And I don't want to transfer, like, the whole thing over to my little net, my little laptop.
And then it'll confuse, it'll get confusing.
I tried that once.
Like, moving pages around and working on it a little bit here and there.
And the problem is I work on, like, one page and, and then the, um, and then I work on that same page where it's located in the other place and I forget.
And that's a disaster.
Because that means that a lot of work is totally gone.
So, yeah, when I do my work, I want it to be on my notebook, my laptop.
And I can't work at night.
So I have to be able to sleep.
So, that's kind of the bummer with that.
I can, I can lie down and, uh, listen to my history of Rome I've been listening to.
But, um, it's just, I don't know.
That just wasn't so easy for me.
It was just stressing me out.
So that's kind of the difficulty for me, living in a van.
Um, and another interesting thing that happened to me is that I took some time off from my routine where I was just eating canned vegetable, which didn't last very, which was just getting started.
Because before that, I was eating rice, which took forever to cook and I didn't like that.
But now I'm doing the canned vegetables and so I'm excited about that.

But, but I, I reverted back into, because it was the holiday season and I just, I don't know what happened, but I just started eating like, uh, burritos and McDonald's and drinking alcohol, which costed a lot more money.
And now I want to get back into my normal routine.
And last night I was up, it was like three in the morning and I was just like bored.
So I walked into the, uh, Walmart, which was right there, the parking lot I was parked in.
And I got, I got nerds and, um, Cheetos.
So just by eating junk food for a couple of days, it got me addicted to it.
Like a crackhead, like a, like an alcoholic, you know, an alcoholic who hasn't drinking for 12 years doesn't have a problem not drinking.
They can be in a part of the room of all these people drinking and they don't, they don't have any urge to drink.
Like, but an alcoholic who's just relapsed last night, well, yeah, now they're going to have the urge to drink.
Uh-oh.
So it's kind of like that.
Um, but, uh, yeah.
So, um, but yeah, the difficult part about, for it to me is, uh, yeah, if I get, if I get, uh, a peer.
And then I wake up and then an hour goes by and now it's dark and I know I'm going to be up all night and I'm going to be sleeping all day.
That's frustrating.
And it wouldn't be frustrating if I lived in a house because I could just be laying in bed, doing the internet, plugged into my computer and all that.
Just makes it a little bit more difficult.
Um, but that's me.
Uh, and I've been doing it a while.
Well, the difficult thing about living in a van, if you just move into the van, is, uh, well, like I said, you're going to be, uh, used to having all those cravings.
And you're not going to be very happy, um, filling up your car with all the garbage from, like, fast food or plastic.
Plastics from packaged food, if that's what you eat, like, you know, even if it's, like, cereal.
It just fills up and then you have to throw it in.
I don't know.
Um, little things like that.
Um, lonely.
I think that's a thing that people worry about.
Lonely.
Do you get lonely?
Um, well, I don't get lonely.
I don't even get close to get lonely, dude.
But I have shit going on in my life.
I have goals.
I have hobbies.
I have a job.

Um, but I guess if you're the kind of person, if you're not, if you were, like, raised, if your parents neglected you and you don't understand the concept of work, like, you grew up playing video games and hanging out with your friends and all of a sudden you're living in a car and you don't know what to do with yourself.
Um, what you should do, I guess it would be hard.
It would be, you would be lonely.
I, what I suggest, the first thing that you do is make sure that you're a daytime person.
Don't be up at night because there's nobody around when you're at night.
If you're dependent on being happy from being, having lots of people around you, um, you, uh, you should be up during the day because there's people around you.
Um, and, uh, and then try to develop hobbies.
Um, like if you're on a road trip and you get like a job at some town and you don't know anybody and, uh, you just, or you don't have a job, you just hang out in your van.
Um, just, um, try to, try to, to get, become passionate about something that takes time.
Like go to the gym, work out four hours a day.
Everybody wants to be fit.
Um, read, watch YouTube videos, listen to audio books.
I could send you my audio book collection.
If you send me an external, um, there's plenty of stuff to do.
You just have to, you just have to do it.
You just have to believe in yourself and do it.
Um, but, uh, yeah, for me, that's the hard part.

And of course, also, I guess the stress, if you don't have money, if you're, if you're like on a road trip and you're trying to, to stretch your money out, um, it can be stressful, but that's only when you spend money.
If you go a few days where you're not spending anything, you're going to pat yourself on the back and you're going to be very excited about the possibilities.
Like, like, oh my God, I only spent $3 today and I have a thousand dollars.
Oh my God, oh, the possibilities.
Think about how long I could survive this way.
Yay.
And then you're going to be excited.
So, um, yeah, but that's really the hardest thing about living in a van.
Um, I don't get lonely, but I do interact.
I do interact with people like on YouTube.
I have comments every day.
I interact with people, um, on like a bunch of different videos, a bunch of videos.
I have, I get multiple comments on every single day, like five comments on a day.
I get, and a lot of those videos are people who don't like me, but I'm still interacting with them.
I'm still commenting.
Yeah.
So that's the hard part for me is just the whole getting my, I am on, I am on night schedule and there's nothing I can do.
I've tried everything I've tried, but, uh, I think the best way to push through that is just to be relaxed and just not care.
Just be cool to be chill.
That's just what's going on.
I think I've got a headache.
I have like a fever.
I think maybe, uh, cosmic rays, the cosmic rays are readjusting my DNA.
That's just how it is, man.
Sometimes you just can't help it.
You just go through bad cycles.
I'm in the gym parking lot right now.
I'm going to go into the gym and work out until 10.
And then I'm going to go and lie down to listen to my history of Rome all night long until sunrise until 430 and hopefully go to the Starbucks at 430.
I was up at 430 today and the only reason I didn't go is because I guess I was stupid, but I should have gone.
So, so, uh, yeah, that's that.
The hard part about living in a van is, uh, there's no, there's no electricity or internet in the car.
Okay.
